Uncertainty and unpredictability of the war between Russia and Ukraine made corporations vulnerable. With the majority of the world turning against the country, imposing sanctions rapidly and creating new legislations, Russian people and businesses were first to suffer.  Astec Industries as a company which imports road construction machinery from the United States, was quickly faced with a challenge. Not only it became harder to ship the machinery into Russia, but their US supplier simply refused to sell the machinery to them. Being heavily dependent on this supplier, the only way for the company to further operate is to establish a new subsidiary in Dubai.  In this paper, my job will be to develop strategic recommendation for establishment of the subsidiary in Dubai, consulting and analyzing what new business opportunities of this foreign market entry can Astec Industries benefit from.  Relevance Relevance of this paper is viewed through the fact that  due to new sanctions, the company is faced with import problem s  of road construction machinery  and wishes to establish a new subsidiary in Dubai to manage those challenges  and expand their overseas operations .  Goal   The goal of this paper is to  develop  foreign market expansion  recommendations   for “Astec Industries” new subsidiary in Dubai.  The objectives   To give a background of Astec Industries  history,  business activities ,  internal environment ; To analyze Russian road construction machinery equipment industry  and Astec Industries position in the market ;     To explain the reasoning behind the decision of a new subsidiary establishment in Dubai ;   To determine how a subsidiary in Dubai can be created and what influence will it have on the company’s operations ;   To a ss ess the attractiveness of  UAE  road construction market  and  analyze the  e xternal environment  factors ;   To choose the appropriate foreign market entry mode ; To establish  strategic recommendation for the marketing strategy ;     To   perform   a   financial   assessment   of   establishing   a   subsidiary ;   To conduct the sensitivity analysis ; To determine the possible risks ; Research object and subject   The research object of this paper is the Astec Industries company, and the subject is the  strategy of subsidiary development  in Dubai.  Characteristics of research methodology   The research methodology of the paper includes both qualitative and quantitative research methods. The qualitative  method  is based on the literature reviews of experts  in  road construction industry  of  both cities, and the interviews of the company’s employees that help understand more in-depth industries insights. The second method is gathering statistical data that will confirm certain assumptions that are highlighted in this paper.   Part 1 Astec Industries history   Founded in 1972, Astec Industries , Inc.  is one of United States leading manufacturer of crushing and screening equipment, cement and asphalt concrete plants, and road construction machinery. Based in  Chattanooga the company quickly established itself as a dominant player on the market by focusing on technological innovation in infrastructure related industries.  In the 90s Russia was under severe change and development, with the demand for infrastructure escalating.  The future owner of Astec Industries saw the potential in those turbulent times and began to explore it possibilities. At the start, he was buying road construction machinery in the US and reselling them in Russia. However, quickly the demand grew and the two parties decided to form an alliance, where the newly formed Russian company would have exclusive rights to sell the US machinery. In 1997 based in Saint Petersburg the company “Astec Industries” was formed as an official representative in Russia and  the C ommonwealth of Independent States (CIS)  countries . The company gives its customers a  wide  range of services, starting from the  selection and purchase, and finishing with post-warranty services. Over more than two decades, Astec Industries corporation covered more than 100 Russian regions and exported products to   Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, and Belorussia.    Astec Industries competencies   Astec Industries offers a variety of products that can be divided into three main categories:  crushing and screening equipment, cement and asphalt concrete plants, and road construction machinery . The crushing and screening equipment represents all different equipment for branches of mining industry, such as stationary, movable, and mobile crushing and sorting plats, washing equipment,  hydraulic hammers , conveyer lines, and mining machines.  Cement and asphalt concrete plants are self-explanatory, while their basic functions are production and preservation of the hot asphalt mixture. Also they recycle certain emissions and can be developed as mobilized plants for higher efficiency.  The road construction machinery has a core function of construction and repair of road facilities.  The product range includes asphalt  pavers which are the ones laying out the asphalt mixture on the ground surface of roads. Cold planers grind and remove the asphalt surface of the roads. Finally, the Shuttle Buggy which overloads the hot asphalt mixture form trucks into the asphalt pavers.  In this paper I will be focusing solely on the road construction machinery branch of the company. Therefore,  it is important to further elaborate on each piece of machinery and explain their mechanics. As of the year 2021, the company has sold over 200 pieces of road construction machinery. The majority share goes to the material transfer vehicles Shuttle Buggy (132 units), then comes the cold planers (41 units), and the asphalt pavers (29 units).  The Shuttle Buggy is the most  demanding product for a reason, its uniqueness, compatibility, and usefulness. It falls into the category of   asphalt concrete loaders which can be of three types. The first and the second types, roller pick-ups and non-agitated material reloaders, don’t fully solve the elimination of segregation and continuous stacking issues. While the third type, anti-segregation material reloader perfectly checks both boxes. Its main function is to reload the asphalt mixture from the truck into the asphalt paver, which solves the continues stacking issue. Secondly, and most importantly, when the mixture enters the vehicle, it had a special rotator which mixes the asphalt from the truck eliminating the segregation problem.  This is detrimental to achieve the best road pavement possible, since when the truck with the asphalt mixture arrives ,  the temperature of that mixture is not evenly spread, the center is warmer, while the outer layers are significantly colder, ranging from 5-20 degree  Celsius  difference.  The service life of an asphalt pavement is dependent on two factors: the homogeneous cavity of the pavement and the evenness of the pavement. The Shuttle Buggy evens out the temperature of the asphalt mixture and then sends it into the asphalt paver.  Therefore, making the pavement both homogeneous and even, since the mixture that is being laid out is temperature wise proportionally distributed.  On the contrary, if there are signs of segregation, the pavement will have different density depending on the temperature and when the roller compresses the pavement, the surface will not be even. A slight difference in the beginning will become very noticeable after few years of exploitation, due to the changes in weather .  All in all, the studies have shown that asphalt reloaders like Shuttle Buggy doubles the lifespan of the road surface.   Moreover,  the high-speed staking technology allows:  To increase the evenness of the asphalt pavement by 2-3 times  To save the amount of mixture needed for the pavement by 2-3 times  The cold planer that the company supplies  also  has its own competitive advantages. It has a unique operational system, where it doesn’t only remove the roads surface and sends the gravel back to the truck, but also performs a function of a cold recycler. This means that its internal drum  rotation can be reversed, making the drum crush the pavement into much smaller pieces.  This mechanism allows those smaller pieces to be added to the asphalt mixture in the Shuttle Buggy and consequently  be  reused on the spot. As a result, this recycling mode lowers the cost of construction or repairs by 44% on average based on internal statistics gathered by the company.  This unique feature does not apply on the regular cold planer, it comes as a separate model and is on average 15% more expensive. The first branch is the road construction department that is dedicated towards asphalt pavers, cold planers, and the Shuttle Buggy. The second branch focuses on screening and crushing equipment, which also requires the additional presence of construction experts. Both of the branches not only sell the products, but also spare parts and provide other related services for maintenance. Third branch consists of internal and external operators  that are at the company’s disposal. Fourth branch is solely comprised of company’s internal processes, whilst the last is dedicated to all services that Astec Industries has to offer.  Fig. 1. Astec Industries Organizational Structure  Astec Industries  has a strong corporate culture which reflects its mission and consists of 8 pillars that are divided under two categories – values and goals. Its values are respect, openness, reliability, and development.  While the goal pillars are trust, partnerships, effective solutions, and reputation. All those pillars can be reflected under one umbrella focusing on  building  long lasting relationships with the clients t o  bring the maximum efficiency and  as a result greater   financial outcome to the company.  Astec Industries corporate culture has created a two decade partnership with VAD that are the biggest customer of the company and still continue to  purchase new equipment to this day.  It is just one of many examples of how rewarding the company’s approach is that transitions to a department that is crucial for any post purchase operation activities.      The service department of the company is a very important branch worth highlighting. Not to undermine the other departments, simply the machinery that the company supplies is very complex and often requires a lot of attention. Astec Industries focuses on service maintenance to stress the reliability and efficiency of supplied equipment operation status. When a unit of machinery is purchased the company sends a specialist to perform the following tasks:  Supervised installation and start-up of machinery  Operation adjustments Personnel operation and maintenance training  If there is a repair issue, the estimations are done on the line with the customer or in presence, with a fast response time. Additionally, as the service specialists finds the source of the problem, the customer gets informed of the following steps needed to  take.    Operational problems are overseen, with means of protection of equipment from environmental exposure and allowable operation mode.  The technicians are operating across regions and countries in order to perform all the necessary repairs and maintenance . The high level of company’s maintenance is associated not only with the skillset and qualification of the service personnel, but also with the  professional development course provided by the manufacturing plant which the personnel attends every two years. Furthermore, every winter, the plant conducts advanced training courses in after-sales service for mechanics of Astec Industries customers .  That include ‘Paving Professional Workshop’ courses, where industry professionals talk about the novelties of the road construction industry, in depth reviewing the use of modern technology for laying asphalt concrete pavements and its compaction. Finally, they go over the laboratory studies of the properties of the finished pavements for better illustration of novelties, mechanics, and other industry  relevancies .     Russian road construction equipment  market   Russian Federation is the largest country in the world with over 17 million square kilometers that respectively reflect 11 percent of the total world mass land.  The length of public roads in Russia according to the  Rosstat  by the end of 2021 had reached over 1.5 million kilometers.  In the beginning of 21st century this number slightly above 500 thousand kilometers, meaning that over the course of two decades one million kilometers of roads has been built so far and considering the vast territory of Russian Federation this is far from boarder line.  To examine closer the amount of funds that has been allocated towards road construction, the data posted below is taken from the Astec Industries marketing department and is divided into two sections, the regional and federal segments. In the road industry both construction and repair orders come from the government that creates a state tenders for the execution of those necessary works. On the regional level it is done through the administration of the subject’s committee responsible for transport infrastructure development. The federal level is ruled by the Federal Road Agency ( Rosavtodor ) and the Ministry of Transport ( Mintrans ) that are responsible parties for road transport industry oversight.  Below is displayed a chart that represents the budget Russian Federation spends yearly on road construction and repairs starting from 2015 and up to the April 14th 2022. After a neutral transition to 2016 ever since there was a steady and healthy upward trend all the way up to 2020, with an average of 18% across those 4 years. In 2021 the amount of funds allocated by the government had remained the same, however, to look closer into each specific segment the following is worth mentioning. The ratio of regional to federal orders in 2020 have been 1759 to 435 respectively, while in the year 2021 it was 2079 to 441. From this data it can be concluded that the government is focusing more on the road construction development within the regions more, whilst maintaining their spending on the federal scale moderate. Finally, over the course of three and a half months in 2022 the allocation exceeds 300 billion Rubles, which in forecast will reach similar levels as the previous two years. According to the  Chairman of the State Duma Committee on Transport and Transport Infrastructure Development Yevgeny  Moskvichev , the state plans to allocate over 340 billion Rubles from the federal budget for the construction and maintenance of the road network in 2022.  This number exactly reflects how much the state  allocated the funds from its federal budget in 2021. Additionally, Vladimir Putin instructed to allocate at least 120 billion Rubles for the reginal road network development by the middle of May.  Currently this number exceeds the minimal allocation by 80 billion, from which can be concluded that at least 600 billion Rubles would be spent from the regional budget into this segment, taking into consideration the current geopolitical situation the country is in. Road Construction Budget Chart for Russian Federation The general overview of the industry has to be compared to the road construction machinery sales statistics to better understand what the above patters mean for the market Astec Industries operates in.   According to the Association of European Businesses press release the sales of road construction equipment in Russia since 2017 are steadily increasing.  That year the growth rate in the industry was 49%, while in the following year the momentum dropped to 31%, and in 2019 the increase was around 12%. In 2020 due to the emergence of global pandemic creating massive problems with supply chain routs and high level of panic, there was a mere difference in sales volume at a 0.3% increase. However, by the end of 2021 there was a prominent 47% increase in sales of new construction equipment. As of Q1 of 2022 the data shows that the demand pressure for the equipment retraced, resulting in a 27% decrease in volume in comparison to the Q4 of 2021. This is a result of the Russian current geopolitical  situation, which has a similar effect as the pandemic, creating fear, uncertainty, and finally overall challenges for the imports of construction equipment.  Following the fund allocation i n  relation to the sales numbers for Q1 of 2022 it is important to reflect on the import stagnation, where Astec Industries operations are an example of many. Certain amount of equipment is currently waiting to be delivered through alternative routes being one of the reasons for a 27% decrease. The federal and regional budget allocation supports the ongoing prosperity of the industry and overall pace of infrastructure development that will continue to grow nonetheless, keeping the demand for equipment at familiar levels.  Company’s position in the market   The previous section of the report that was dedicated to  analyzing Russian road construction market, reflect ing  the ongoing demand for the equipment that is correlated to the infrastructure development.  Astec Industries is one of many companies that supplies road construction equipment, amongst its main competitors are  “Vogele”,  “Wirtgen Group”,   “Volvo”,   “XCMG” ,  “Dynapac”,  “ Bomag ”, “Caterpillar”,   and  “Weiler” .  All these competitors supply asphalt pavers, road cutters , and cold planers, as their main products. Some companies like “Volvo” and “Wirtgen Group” have a wide product range including rather small scaled asphalt pavers, cold planers, soil stabilizers, milling machines and more.   According to the statistics gather by Astec Industries the supply for the road construction machinery is on a healthy rise after the covid recession. The following statistics only covers the three product lines which the company supplies, that is asphalt pavers, cold planers, and anti-segregation reloaders. Overall, in 2019 the total amount of equipment supplied was estimated to be worth 119 million USD, while in the following year the volume decreased by 21% reaching 94.5 million USD. However, as the global economy began slowly recovering from the pandemic in 2021 the demand reached its highest numbers closing the year at a mark of 140 million USD which is a 48%  increase.  Further expanding on the quantity supplied across those years, in 2019 a total of 524 units were supplied, where Astec Industries  accounted for 8 . Important to mention, that there was one asphalt paver, two cold planers, and five  shuttle buggies .  In the following year only 454 units were supplied out of which only six were  Astec’s  and all reloaders. Finally, in 2021 with a surplus to 669 units, out of which three cold planers and  nine  shuttle buggies were from the company.  Overall, the biggest supplier of the reloaders for the market was Astec Industries, varying from  37.5 % to  75 % due to the competitive advantage its shuttle buggies have to other reloaders.  The cold planers that were supplied were all hybrid, meaning they acted not only as a cold planer, but also as a recycler, another competitive advantage product the company has.  However, due to the high end cost of the machinery, ranging between 750 and 800 thousand USD, it is not very competitive and only big construction companies are willing to pay that price.  Wirtgen Group is the leader in terms of sales volume for the cold planers and recyclers, that are two separate  equipment for them, with a market share varying from 45 up to 58 percent, due to a significantly lower equipment cost with an estimated average of  4 00,000 USD. The asphalt pavers market is dominated by Vogele company with a 30% average share, whilst Astec Industries over those three years was able to sell only one asphalt paver due to its high end price and lack of any advantage over the competitors that comes with it.  Company’s decision on establishment  of a new subsidiary in Dubai   Since February 24 th   2022 , when Russia began its invasion into Ukraine, European Union, United States and other countries began imposing sanctions on Russia.  Most export and import duties were either blocked or paused due to the war.  Astec Industries being a company that is heavily dependent on its US supplier quickly began experiencing those supply chain challenges. The American corporation refused to sell and ship any machinery due to fear of creating a backlash from its shareholders.  Six units of machinery, to be specific five Shuttle Buggies and one cold planer, were put on hold.  The renewal of the shipment entirely depends on the geopolitical situation and the sanctions, where there are too many unknowns to predict when this will end. On the contrary, if a subsidiary was established, the US company would have no problem shipping the equipment to that intermediary, as what values to them is who customer and what is the operating jurisdiction.  Even though Astec Industries in the beginning of 2022 terminated the exclusive dealers agreement with the American company, it still  remained  its  biggest supplier. Therefore, as customers in Russia were waiting for the machinery to be shipped while  it was stuck in the port, Astec Industries  wasn’t ready to simply wait for the conflict resolution and  c a me up with a  decision  to open a new subsidiary in Dubai. The company chose this rout e  due to several reasons: Efficiency:  The  Dubai  subsidiary will provide Astec Industries with a new front end company for the renewal of any business alliance  f rom the supply chain standpoint.  Moreover, the company is not obligated to use the facilities provided by the Dubai  marina hub, making it possible to use other intermediary transshipment ports for the machinery shipments.    Entry barrier:  O pen ing  a company in Dubai doesn’t require a lot of finances and additionally  it takes a month to set it up.  Consequently, the costs of opening a subsidiary and maintaining it will be quickly overthrown by the fact that the  supply of machinery will be resumed.    Taxes: Dubai is called a tax heaven for a reason. Any activity that a company performs which does not harm the internal economy in Dubai is tax free. If the company sells its goods or services on the territory of Dubai that will be taxable, anything outside, where the city acts as an intermediary, is totally free  of taxes .     Astec Industries  objective is to establish an international trading company in Dubai in order to utilize the corporate structure for international trade performance. The International Free Zone Authority (IFZA) is a hub which was developed for the United Arab Emirates that offers “ highly customized approach combined with effective international service standards and cost-effective solution s.”  The main benefits of the IFZA are the following:  100% ownership of commercial enterprises  100% repatriation of Capital and Profit 100% Import and Export tax exemptions No personal income tax A safe and business-friendly legal framework for Corporates  Full flexibility for upgrading of License (Like more employees / More Residence Visa / Office etc.) This serves as a perfect ground for Astec Industries to establish a subsidiary which will enable the company to continue its long lasting relationship with the US manufacturer, as well as, expand the business opportunities as a whole.  According to   the compan y Mizar Management Consultancy s etting up a company requires an investment of approximately 1 0 ,000 USD that include license, immigration card for the local nominee director, Flexi desk office agreement, one residence visa, and a national ID. Furthermore,  the  annual renewal cost f or  the license will be around 9,500 USD and additional 3,000 USD for residence visa extension. As for the duration, the whole process from start to  finish will take around 2 months, while the company will be able to begin its operations  after one month.  Finally, the US company manufacturer has never operated on the UAE market as it focuses on the North and South America continents, as well as, Asia with China as the biggest supplier. The manufacturer has a much wider product line, that encompasses not only road construction machinery, but also asphalt and cement concrete plants, and crushing and screening equipment. Based on the data provided to me by the Russian company, the manufacturer focuses mainly on plants, and research and development branch to continuously innovate their products. Finally, there will be no competition on the UAE market for the Russian company, as the US manufacturer agreed to not engage into the market where Astec Industries is operating while their exclusive dealers agreement is not fully terminated.  Taking everything above into account means that Astec Industries will be able to move forward with the equipment that is frozen in the  US port, resolving its primary goal. Important to mention that the US company gave Astec Industries half a year to either come up with an alternative solution or the equipment will be delisted and sold to someone else. This perspective puts Astec Industries reputation at stake, since if they were to place a new order that can also be done only if the global response to Russian action deescalates, it will take at least another 9 months for that equipment to arrive to a client in Russia.  Essentially this is a turning  point for the Astec Industries to either solve the matter quickly, which is what the company is focusing on, or  potentially  los e  their biggest supplier along with some clients that specifically approached  or were in relations with  the company for that brands equipment.  Secondly, it brings new opportunities to the company, as Astec Industries can utilize the potential of UAE market, not only as a front solution, but also as a new market to establish their operations in. Exactly this will be examined in the following part through analyzing the UAE market based on the PESTEL and Porter’s Five Forces analyses.  Part 2   Analysis of the  UAE  market   Over the past two decades  UAE  has grown from a mild desert  state  into one of the most lucrative places to live in and a global financial hub.  Containing of seven Emirates, UAE due to their massive hydrocarbon reserves became the leading country in the Gulf region. Currently UAE is seeking to reduce its dependency on oil and gas and the recent reports published by the government show positive signs. The non-oil sector contribution towards GDP accounted for 72.3% in 2021, which is 3.8% increase for the 2020 year after the covid restrictions and travel challenges were eased.  The biggest contributors to the non-oil sector were hotels and restaurants with 21.3%, retail at 14.1%, and health & social services at 13.8%. UAE economy grew by 3.8 per cent last year, beating World Bank’s forecast of 2.1 per cent, and is expecting 5.4 per cent growth this year according to the Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank. Driven by robust government policies, Abu Dhabi’s economy is showing strong signs of recovery from the pandemic. The emirate’s non-oil economy grew by 4.1% last year with agriculture, forestry and infrastructure sectors impacting the rise.  T he second wealthiest  e mirate Dubai is a very important trade and tourist destination of the Gulf  state . Its port  JebeL  Ali operates as the center for most of export trade in the Middle East.  Dubai International Airport in Q4 of last year recorded its busiest quarter since the pandemic outbreak, with passenger count reaching 11.8 million, driven by Expo 2020 Dubai.   Introducing Dubai International Financial Center (DIFC) in 2004, the city quickly developed into a global hub for IT service industries  and is planning to become the regional leader in artificial intelligence after announcing its AI license program.  The  Dubai e mirate has relatively small oil reserves, so it focuses its long-term growth strategy on the development of   non-oil sector  and economy diversification, just like UAE . The funds received from the export of oil resources are used to finance the tourism, hospitality, trade, transport, logistics, and financial services industries .   As one of the fastest growing cities in the world Dubai ’s  population is increasing by 10.7% annually when analyzing the data for the past 5  years.  In recent light of the conflict between Russia and Ukraine, the migration of Russian citizens to Dubai h as exploded and in the first 10  days  it is estimated that roughly 150,000 people came to the global city.  The increase in population and tourism Dubai keeps expanding its infrastructure to create lucrative investment opportunities  from outside and within the city. This consequently brings demand to the road construction sector, as the city cannot expand solely on the territory that is already put to use.  On top of that,  Dubai was set to host the 2020 E xpo  but due to the pandemic crisis it was opened a year later and lasted for  6 months , closing at the end of March of 2022.  Over 25 million people have visited the E xpo  over the course of 6 months, massively strengthening Dubai and UAE  economically .  According to the study  performed   by  Ernst  & Young more than 900,000 jobs were generated in the region and it is expected that it will boost UAE economy by 33.4 billion USD by 2031.   For the calculation, the following factors were taken into account: "direct" growth in economic activity, "indirect" benefits from increased demand for supply chains and "induced" benefits from increased purchasing power of employees of firms involved in Expo   2020. Since 2017 the value of construction contracts awarded in the UAE has decrease by half, with pandemic  in 2020  driving the percent down to 24.76% in comparison to the previous year of 43.23%.  Yet   in  the most recent update in 2021 construction contracts increased to 30.61% , and are projected to continue the uptrend with an average 4% over the next years .   According to BNC group report it is estimated that by 2025  over 2,200  transportation projects will be developed across UAE. As the second largest construction market in the Gulf Cooperation Council with around 800 billion USD worth of ongoing and planning projects, transportation sector accounts for 13%.  Consequently, on average the road construction market in UAE would account for 26 billion USD yearly, until 2026.  Recently, at a cost of 1.9 billion Dirhams three  new major roads were opened.  Their main purpose was to cut the travel time and traffic flow improvement, connecting Dubai, Ajman, and Ras Al Khaimah.   Dubai has many upcoming infrastructure projects  set to complete in the following decade, such as MBR City, Dubai Mall of the World, and Meydan One to name a few.  As for UAE, there is an overload of future project like E-15 Highway, AI-Muntazi 2, AI- Banya  district categorized as scalable, however, every emirate is producing large amount of tenders for construction and maintenance.  All the above information presents a peripherally view of a healthy economy and rapid growth pace  that  Astec Industries can take to its advantage. In order to further analyze the attractiveness of the market the following two analysis PESTEL and Porter will be conducted in this part of the paper. It consists of seven emirates – Dubai, Abu Dhabi,  Fujairah, Ajman, Sharjah, Umm Al Quwain, and Ras Al Khaimah.   Each state has a ruler and together they form a Federal Supreme Council, the highest constitutional authority of the Emirates.  Also, the council with seven rulers appoints one member to be the President of UAE.   Apart from this ruling system that is very narrow and centric, the government also doesn’t allow for parties to be established.  This limited civil liberty gives the heredity rules all the influence and power across UAE. Consequently,  this factor affects the stability for most of the  industr ies  operating in UAE due to such narrow power distribution and dependency on seve n  individuals dictating the future of Emirates.  C urrently UAE with the Dubai trade hub are portrait by the foreign investors as a very attractive market to invest in  and it is forecasted to continue on this trajectory and increase by each year. However, it cannot be neglected that    the limited political freedom definitely raises awareness  and brings certain risks to the market, requiring companies to stay flexible and have high adaptive abilities.  Growing  R egional  P ower   UAE is considered to be one of the most powerful and influential country in the Middle East. It has established itself as the main trade center in the Gulf region with Dubai having the highest transit flow of trade amongst it. It formed diplomatic relations with powerful countries like United States, China, India, Russia, Saudi Arabia and many other.  Over the past UAE had conflicting relations with Iran over the Yemen war. However, Iran being one of the lead players in the region UAE  recently  de-escalated its tensions with the country to  create mutual security and focus on economic needs.  In a quest of becoming more independent whilst maintaining the power in Gulf, UAE established full diplomatic relations with Israel, which led to an influx of investments and tourism from Israeli people.  Due to  the abovementioned influential diplomatic relations UAE was able to shift its economy towards a non-oil based domination.  Advantageous Tax Policies  Dubai  is viewed as one of the most lucrative placed to live in and perform economic activity due to its tax policies. There is no personal income tax in Dubai and in the whole UAE, however some businesses have to pay taxes. All the oil businesses are heavily taxed with a 55% rate and all the income of foreign banks operating in the UAE are taxable with a 20% rate. Also, UAE stated that since the beginning of 2023 fiscal year federal corporate income tax (CIT)  at 9%  will be introduced.  However, this will only apply to the businesses that operate within the country  and have an income of over 375,000 AED . If a company is established in Dubai for international business operations, the 100% repatriation of capital and profit along with a 100% import and export tax exemptions will remain, as it operates in the International Free Zone Authority. This is one of the driving factors for  UAE  continuous economic growth, attracting not only employees, but also companies to establish their operations on its territory.   Economic factors   High  GDP  per capita  UAE is one of the top countries in terms of GDP and per capita income according to the Statista with 410 billion USD as of 202 1 ranking 29th globally  and this number is projected to increase by 11% over the next 5 years.   Out of this, the gulf country per capita income was 43,537 dollars, being 19th world’s highest. Strategic recommendations for  entering the Dubai market   After identifying that the best market entry mode for Astec Industries would be greenfield investment, it is now necessary to develop strategic recommendations for this entry mode. In this section a theoretical framework called 4P will be used to develop those recommendations. The 4P’s a re  the four pillars that the framework is based on and they are “product”, “price”, “place”, and “promotion.”   The first pillar refers to the products or services that the company has to offer. Price is how much customers are paying in order to get those products or services. Place refers to the “how” a customer can purchase them and “how” they are distributed. Lastly, promotion stands for the channels through which the company is reaching its potential clients.  Product  In the road construction business, Astec Industries sells three products : c old planers, Shuttle Buggies, and asphalt pavers. Cold planers are in charge of removing the road surface and transporting it to the truck. The distinctive feature of their planers is the capacity to reverse the rotation of the drums, allowing the machine to crush the asphalt into smaller pieces that may then be reused  right on sight . This is known as recycler machinery, which is produced by other firms but as a distinct product, whereas Astec Industries combines them into a single cold recycling planer.   Shuttle Buggy is a one-of-a-kind piece of equipment that reloads the asphalt mixture from the truck into the paver. Its primary function is to mix the asphalt delivered by the track before it enters the asphalt paver, ensuring that the temperature of the asphalt is consistent after transit. Because the asphalt  closer to the boarders of the truck  cools  down  faster than the mixture in the center , t his method is required to ensure that the road pavement is even and has a long lifespan.  Finally, a sphalt pavers are the well-known machines that lay out the asphalt uniformly,  creating the final road pavement .  Outlining the value propositions for each machinery type is of vital importance to recognize and further communicate it to prospective customers. In this scenario a warehouse should be chosen closer to the port, so that the company will not be paying extra for the shipment our of its own budget in advance. Jebel Ali is area where a lot of warehouses are concentrated, with prices ranging from 110,000 AED up to millions, depending on the square foot size.    As for the purchasing mechanism of machinery, it will follow the same procedure as in Russia.  When a firm and a customer reach an agreement through the sales department, the order is sent to the manufacturer, who defines the date of shipment and returns an invoice. The client pays the invoice amount, the paperwork is returned to the manufacturer, and the transaction is sealed. Following customs clearance in  Dubai port , the items would be transported by road directly to the appointed destination.   In case of a warehouse scenario, the procedure will be similar, but simply performed in advance, where the customer will be Astec Industries itself.  Finally,  accounting for  the two factor characteristics of transit time and price, road shipping will be the most appropriate transportation option. When comparing road and train transportation methods, the price is  similar , but the travel time varies dramatically. This is because, while the shipping goods is attached to the carriage, its journey is not from the place of origin to the point of delivery. The carriage makes multiple stops where other items are transshipped, which might take a long time. Also, because this cannot be entirely accounted for, the goods may be delayed if sent by railroad. Promotion  Promotion is the final pillar of the marketing mix, and it is critical for the company's income streams. The newly founded firm in Dubai would use  similar  advertising methods as in Russia. First and foremost, a website displaying all of the firm's  machinery units, communicating their value propositions  and other key information about the company.  Astec Industries  should use the following promotion tactics  as their main communication channels .  Firstly, exercise  2B2 mailing  promotion   where  the firm would examine the mark et , locat e  companies who would be interested in and require their machinery , and reach out to them with displaying value propositions of each equipment unit .  Secondly, i t is also critical to attend road construction exhibits such as " The Roads and Traffic Expo "  and “Gulf Traffic Exhibition.”  This is an excellent technique for  the company to display its competitive products to the mass target customer segment. Additionally, the company exercises such promotion strategy in every country it operates and proven by time, it is the most effective channel to engage in.  Finally, the firm should publish papers in both physical and online themed publications. This helps to widen the reach of advertising since road construction businesses are constantly looking for new creative technologies in the sector to get a competitive  edge over its rivalry. Risk Assessment   Entering the UAE road construction equipment market through greenfield investments presents certain risks. Therefore, it is necessary to examine factors that may disrupt company’s operations on the market. In this section a risk assessment matrix will be compiled to understand what issues may disturb the subsidiary operations based on 2 criteria’s, the likeliness of the risk occurrence and its impact significance on the firm.  The following risks can be identified for the company’s operations:  Exclusive dealers agreement termination  This factor will arise in the future when the global political tensions with Russian Federation will deescalate and the US manufacturer will start selling and shipping machinery directly to Russia. Therefore, the exclusive dealers agreement termination will lead to other companies having the ability to supply the equipment produced by the US manufacturer. This is fact leads Astec Industries to no competitive advantage over the market since someone else can be selling the same machinery and potentially lowering prices due to bigger scalability, dominating the niche which once was company’s empty market.  Significant price increase of machinery  The suppliers power proves to be a very significant factor for Astec Industries. Currently relying solely on one single supplier, the US manufacturer can affect the price of machinery by a margin. In the past years the manufacturer has raised prices on average by 3-5%, however, it did not occur regularly. Depending on the economic situation, with the pandemic being the last indicator that affected the price increase. The supplier is not planning on raising prices this year, yet it is not certain that the same will be true for the second half of 2022 or in the following year.    Inability to attract enough customers  Astec Industries due to external factors is provided with an opportunity to expand its international operations and enter the UAE market. As a result of thorough market analysis, the financial valuation has proved the ability for the company to be competitive on the market and most importantly stay profitable.  The company has two unique products which are extremely competitive and the job will be to display that value proposition correctly though different promotional channels. However, there is a risk factor of the company’s inability to sell enough machinery to sustain the Dubai subsidiary. Since it took the Russian company decades to reach the market share and convey the attractiveness of machinery.  Inability to beat the competition  From the Porter’s Five Forces analysis and 4P strategic recommendations, the competitive landscape of UAE was determined and analyzed. Focusing on cold planers and material transfer vehicle segments the comparison showed that price wise the equipment is competitive and has its own unique features. Nevertheless, there is still a risk that Astec Industries will not be able to beat the existing UAE competition, consequently triggering the decline in revenue streams of the company. Risk Assessment Matrix (1-  exclusive dealers agreement termination ,  2  – significant price increase of the machinery,  3 – inability to attract enough customers,  4 –  inability to beat the competition ) Considering different possible risk factors it can be stated that the most vital risk for the company will be the termination of exclusive dealers agreement. It is a matter of time to when that will occur, but since it was set in motion in the beginning of the year, at some stage it will happen. Astec Industries is not planning to operate under the agreement any longer, thus as soon as political tensions with Russian Federation will deescalate, the agreement termination will proceed. Therefore, other companies will be able to supply machinery units of the US manufacturer onto the UAE market. It is impossible to predict when the termination will occur, but in any case Astec Industries will be the first company to start supplying those machinery units. This head lead will play a major role for the company, in order to position itself on the market and gain as much attraction towards those units as possible.  Existing players on the UAE market already have a clientele base and company recognition that may disrupt Astec Industries plans to establish itself on the market. Nevertheless, with the time factor, the odds are in the company’s favor and the best mitigation would be to execute on the subsidiary establishment as soon as possible.  Secondly, the US manufacturer increasing prices is another risk factor that has to be addressed. There is no specific schedule that the manufacturer follows, but on average when there is an increase in price, it ranges between 3 and 5 per cent. As for the duration in between, previously it occurred for semi-annually to once every two years, where the basis of that is the global economic state. The company’s machinery units are relatively high priced, especially when analyzing the asphalt paver segment, which is the reason for the demand lack for those equipment units. The other two high competitive machinery are currently not too overpriced,  however, with price increases from the manufacturer, that still poses a threat. Astec Industries previously examined profit share reduction strategy when a scenario like this occurred. This will be the main mitigation strategy in UAE as well, if an unexpected price raise arises. However, the company cannot exercise such strategy towards every single customer, since company profit is the main priority. Astec Industries has to examine each customer thoroughly and understand whether they can establish a long term relations with them, in which case such strategy will be suitable. Additionally, it is important to analyze the competitors price actions, where if the whole market is on the rise, there is no need for any cost cutting and special discounts implementations.   Finally, inability to attract enough customers and beat the competition risk factors were not determined to be so probable. The reasoning behind this is the uniqueness of the machinery units that the company will supply based on its operation on the Russian market and a resent foreign expansion to the Kazakhstan market, where the demand exceeded any expectations. Moreover, in a case of exclusive deals agreement termination, as previously stated, the company still has a time advantage over other suppliers that may potentially add the US manufacturing equipment into their product line. Consequently, both these risk factors are not probable, yet the only mitigation if they do occur, would be for the company to use aggressive promotion strategies, potential cost reduction, or simply leave the UAE market. Since direct shipment into Russia will be restored and the company may close the subsidiary without  losing  significant revenue.  Conclusion   The main goal of this consulting project was to develop foreign market expansion recommendations for Astec Industries new subsidiary in Dubai. While working on reaching this goal the following objectives, have been met: To give a background of Astec Industries  history,  business activities , internal environment ; To analyze Russian road construction machinery equipment industry and Astec Industries position in the market ;    To explain the reasoning behind the decision of a new subsidiary establishment in Dubai ;   To determine how a subsidiary in Dubai can be created and what influence will it have on the company’s operations ;   To assess the attractiveness of  UAE  road construction market and analyze the external environment factors ;   To choose the appropriate foreign market entry mode ;   To establish  strategic recommendation for the marketing strategy ;   To   perform   a   financial   assessment   of   establishing   a   subsidiary ;   To conduct the sensitivity analysis ;   To determine the possible risks ;    In the beginning of the paper, Astec Industries origin background and primary business activities were assessed. Following was analyzed the Russian road construction market and machinery equipment industry with competitive landscape positions. Afterwards, it was stated why the company has decided to establish a new subsidiary in Dubai along with a brief overview of what would that procedure look like, according to the Mizar Management Consultancy. The following paper parts included analyzing the attractiveness of the UAE road construction machinery market, choosing the most suitable market entry mode for the company,  developing a marketing strategy and financially evaluating the market entry mode for the subsidiary, conduction of sensitivity analysis and accounting for different risks. The analysis of the UAE market determined several important findings which are to be addressed. PESTEL and Porter’s Five Forces analyses were performed in this paper due to them serving different but crucial purposes for obtaining the market understanding. PESTEL analysis displays how various macro-level environmental factors are to impact Astec Industries subsidiary in the future and strong or weak the subsidiary’s competitive position will be in  relations to those factors on the UAE market.  Porter’s Five Forces analy si s  framework was used to analyze the subsidiary’s. competitive environment, specifically its influence and power to suppliers, buyers, competitors, and new entrants. The following conclusions from the analyses were obtained:  Called a tax heaven,  if a company is established in Dubai for international business operations, there is a 100% repatriation of capital and profit along with a 100% import and export tax exemptions, as it operates in the International Free Zone Authority. Dubai Roads and Transport Authority (RTA) set a five year sustainability plan focusing on global mobility. It plans to encompass several transportation projects supporting the goal of reaching 100% recycling on all RTA assets across UAE by the end of the decade.    As the second largest construction market in the Gulf Cooperation Council with around 800 billion USD worth of ongoing and planning projects, transportation sector accounts for 13%. Consequently, on average the road construction market in UAE would account for 26 billion USD yearly, until 2026. Dubai was globally ranked the number one city in attracting foreign direct investment projects in 2021. It attracted 618 FDI projects including greenfield, reinvestment, and other forms of investments, with a total estimate of 7 billion USD.  Competitive landscape in the UAE is dominated by 7 suppliers that account for over 500 machinery units that were supplier to the market in 2021. Wirtgen Group, Caterpillar, and Volvo Construction Equipment dominate the three product segments that Astec Industries possesses. With an estimate of 15 billion USD allocated to road construction market in UAE, the ratio is exceedingly pleasant for a company to operate in.  From the following analyses and Astec industries preliminary goal of resolving the logistics issues between Russian and United States, it was determined that the best entry mode for the company to engage in would be greenfield investment, creating a new subsidiary in Dubai.  A   theoretical framework called 4P  was applied in order to develop strategic recommendations for the subsidiary. The marketing mix covered future product price evaluation, its distribution channels, and promotion mechanisms for value propositions.  Afterwards, the financial evaluation was performed based on the market size, competitor share distribution, and other market estimations. Also, the valuation included and was estimated for machinery units that will be sold and shipped to Russia.  The PP, DPP ,  NPV,  and  IRR   are  indicators by which th e entry mode  attractiveness was evaluated . As a result, it was clear that the greenfield entry mode for establishing the Dubai subsidiary should be executed.  The sensitivity analysis was performed to further understand how the main target financial performance variables would be affected by different sales volume inputs. The two scenarios, adjusted to 50% and 200% sales volume respectively were examined to determine how dependent the subsidiary will be solely on attracting clients on the UAE market. Finally, the risk assessment was conducted based on different risk factors regarding the Dubai subsidiary. The analysis proved that the biggest risk factor will be the termination of exclusive dealers agreement that will take place as soon as the global political tensions and sanctions deescalate. Where the subsidiary will stop being the only supplier of machinery units in the UAE, lowering its influence and competitive advantage over the market. In recapitulation, it must be stated that the goal of the paper was successfully accomplished, and all the objectives were met.  References   Astecindustries.ru. 2022.  Astec Industries, Inc. . Цели:
1. Оценка экономического развития и потенциала инфраструктурных проектов в Объединенных Арабских Эмиратах.
2. Исследование влияния корпоративного налога на экономику страны.

Задачи:

1. Сбор и анализ данных о росте ВВП, экспорте, инвестициях и других экономических показателях Объединенных Арабских Эмиратов за различные периоды времени.
2. Изучение эффективности и влияния на экономику различных типов инфраструктурных проектов, таких как строительство дорог, мостов, аэропортов и т.д.
3. Оценка влияния введения корпоративного налога на экономику страны, включая анализ налоговой базы, ставок налога и возможных последствий для бизнеса и населения.
4. Разработка прогнозов экономического развития Объединенных Арабских Эмиратов на основе анализа исторических данных и оценки текущих тенденций.
5. Анализ чувствительности прогнозов к изменениям внешних факторов, таким как мировой спрос на энергию, изменение цен на сырье и другие экономические условия.
6. Создание финансовых моделей оценки стоимости проектов и определения их рентабельности.
7. Исследование рынка недвижимости и коммерческой недвижимости в Дубае, включая анализ спроса и предложения, цен на объекты и перспективы развития.
8. Оценка влияния технологических инноваций и цифровизации на экономический рост и развитие Объединенных Арабских Эмиратов.
9. Анализ системы здравоохранения и безопасности труда в Объединенных Арабских Эмиратах и разработка рекомендаций по улучшению условий работы и здоровья граждан.
10. Исследование возможности использования бесплатных зон для стимулирования экономического роста и развития малого и среднего бизнеса в Объединенных Арабских Эмиратах.
